reset_tests.description: No reset signal exists. Verify the module is purely combinational by applying stable inputs from time 0 and checking that `out_valid` and `out` immediately reflect the current inputs with no reset prerequisite and no retained state from prior stimuli.
Change from one valid input pattern to a different valid input pattern without any reset-like operation and verify outputs recompute solely from the new `in`, `shift`, and `fill` values.
Drive an invalid shift (`shift`=5, 6, or 7) and then return to a valid shift, confirming `out_valid` returns to 1 based only on the present inputs and that no stale invalid-state behavior remains.

normal_tests.description: Identity mapping for `shift=0`. Use distinct 5-bit symbols in all 10 lanes so ordering is observable. Expect `out_valid=1` and `out[i]=in[i]` for every symbol position i.
Single-symbol logical right shift for `shift=1`. Expect symbol positions 0..8 to take `in[1]..in[9]` and position 9 to become `fill`.
Mid-range shift for `shift=2`. Expect exactly two most-significant output symbol positions to be filled with the same 5-bit `fill` symbol and all remaining positions to map from `in[i+2]`.
Maximum valid shift for `shift=4`. Expect positions 0..5 to take `in[4]..in[9]` and positions 6..9 to all equal `fill`.

Exhaustive control-space sweep over `shift=0..7` with one fixed highly distinguishable input pattern and one nontrivial `fill`. For `shift<=4`, check the exact symbol mapping formula. For `shift>4`, check only `out_valid=0` and do not constrain `out`.

corner_case_tests.description: Invalid shift boundary. Check `shift=5`, `6`, and `7` explicitly. These must produce `out_valid=0`; `out` is don't-care and must not be overconstrained.
Boundary fills. Use `fill=00000` and `fill=11111` to catch zero-fill assumptions, sign-extension-like behavior, or partial replication bugs.

Per-symbol replication check. Choose a `fill` with mixed bits and a shift inserting multiple vacated lanes. Confirm every vacated 5-bit symbol equals the full `fill` value, not a bitwise smear or truncated pattern.
Direction-disambiguation test. Use unique symbols and a nonmatching fill so right-shift, left-shift, and reversed-order implementations all produce different observable outputs.
Bitwise-vs-symbolwise discrimination. Use adjacent symbols whose concatenation would reveal an erroneous 1-bit or 2-bit shift. Check valid shifts 1 through 4 against exact 5-bit-lane movement only.
Off-by-one source-index detection. Use a monotonic symbol ramp so `in[i+S-1]`, `in[i+S]`, and `in[i+S+1]` are easy to separate. Run for several shifts, especially `shift=1` and `shift=4`.
Packed-bus endianness check. Place distinctive symbols only at the first and last positions, with all interior symbols equal, so swapped symbol-order interpretations become obvious.
Output validity independence from data. Hold `in` and `fill` constant and vary only `shift`; verify `out_valid` changes only according to `shift<=4` and not according to data patterns.

sequence_tests.description: Combinational transition sequence over all legal and illegal shifts using one fixed distinguishable input pattern. Apply `shift` in the order 0,1,2,3,4,5,4,3,2,1,0,7 and verify each step independently from the current inputs only. This catches stale outputs, cached previous values, and wrong validity comparison logic.

Input-change stress sequence with constant `shift=2`. Change only `fill`, then only `in`, then both together; verify `out` immediately follows the formula `out[i]=in[i+2]` for i<=7 and `out[8]=out[9]=fill`. This separates correct combinational recomputation from latent registered-output behavior.

Invalid-to-valid-to-invalid sequence. Apply `shift=6`, then `shift=4`, then `shift=5` while changing `in` and `fill` each step. Check that valid cycles produce exact symbol-mapped outputs and invalid cycles check only `out_valid=0`, preventing accidental dependence on previously constrained `out` values.

random_tests.description: Generate 200 random vectors over the full input space: random 50-bit `in`, random 3-bit `shift`, random 5-bit `fill`. For each vector, if `shift<=4`, compute the oracle per 5-bit symbol as `expected_out[i]=in[i+shift]` for source positions still within 0..9 else `fill`, and require `out_valid=1`. If `shift>4`, require only `out_valid=0` and treat `out` as don't-care. Bias at least half of the random vectors toward discriminator-rich patterns such as unique per-symbol ramps, alternating symbols, all-zeros, all-ones, endpoint-unique patterns, and mixed-bit `fill` values.

key_discriminators: `shift=0` must be a pure identity mapping with `out_valid=1`.
Shifting is by whole 5-bit symbols, not by individual bits.
The operation is a logical right shift in symbol space, not a left shift.
Vacated positions are on the most-significant symbol side, not the least-significant side.
Every vacated symbol slot must equal the full 5-bit `fill` value, replicated per symbol.
`out_valid` depends only on `shift<=4`; data values must not affect validity.
When `out_valid=0`, `out` must not be strictly checked, preventing false failures on unconstrained invalid outputs.
Unique-symbol patterns must be used to detect off-by-one source indexing and packed-bus endianness mistakes.
Back-to-back input changes must update outputs immediately, distinguishing combinational behavior from stale or registered outputs.
